alternatives_considered: Keep adapter-per-runtime as only integration path — rejected: doesn't scale beyond 4-5 runtimes
MCP replaces everything including auto-capture — rejected: MCP has no lifecycle events, can't trigger on /clear or session.end
Wait for MRP adoption — rejected: high friction for runtime maintainers, MCP is already adopted
content.context: Discussion about why MOM wasn't already exposing memory via MCP. Realized: adapter-per-runtime was the first approach (inertia), MCP matured recently, and we underestimated what MCP covers. MCP handles recall + manual write (request-response). MRP still needed for push-based lifecycle hooks (session.end, /clear, /compact).
content.decision: MCP server is the universal entry point for MOM recall. Any MCP-aware runtime connects via config, zero adapter code. Adapters shrink to boot-file generation + MRP hook wiring only.
impact: Adapter surface shrinks ~60%: only boot-file + hooks remain
v0.9 reshuffled: MCP server as primary deliverable instead of dual-output capture
Outreach strategy becomes viable: PRs to external runtimes add MCP config entry, not code
Four integration tiers defined: Tier 0 (bootstrap), Tier 1 (MCP recall), Tier 2 (MCP read+write), Tier 3 (full auto-capture via adapter+MRP)
New architecture doc: 15-mcp-server.md with full spec
content.why: Changes the game from 'runtimes must implement MRP to integrate' to 'runtimes already speak MCP, just plug MOM'. Friction drops from days-of-code to minutes-of-config. Unlocks Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Codex, Continue, Zed, openclaude, etc. simultaneously.
